clusters.description: Patterns for moving binary content from a client to durable storage.

members.summary: Chunked uploads with retry and progress (mobile).
Drag-drop dropzone with preview and S3 direct upload (web).

clusters.similarities: All four hand off bytes to remote storage and care about reliability for slow or interrupted networks. They surface progress to the user (chunked progress callback, dropzone thumbnails, image-manipulator stages, tus events).
clusters.differences: alpha-app and charlie-cli implement chunking/resume themselves; bravo-platform delegates to S3 direct upload; delta-mobile uses Firebase Storage and only does pre-upload size reduction. alpha-app is the only one with a visible queue UI.
clusters.consolidationNote: alpha-app's chunked uploader and charlie-cli's tus streaming both implement resumable transfers — could share an interface even though the wire protocol differs.

clusters.description: Abstractions for choosing between LLM providers per task or capability.

members.summary: Two-provider routing: Claude vs Gemini based on grounding need.
Per-job-type routing across three providers with availability pings.
claude -p shell-out instead of SDK; single provider but bypass-the-API approach.

clusters.similarities: Each project treats the AI provider as a swappable strategy and isolates provider-specific logic in a single module.
clusters.differences: alpha-app routes inline at call site; bravo-platform reads model preference from settings per job type; charlie-cli only uses one provider but via subprocess instead of HTTP SDK — different axis of provider abstraction.
clusters.consolidationNote: alpha-app and bravo-platform's routers could share a Provider interface.

members.summary: AES-GCM over AsyncStorage with derived keys (mobile).
AES-GCM on sensitive columns at the application layer (server).

clusters.similarities: Both use AES-GCM at the application layer rather than relying on transport or DB-level encryption, and both manage their own keys.
clusters.differences: alpha-app encrypts a key/value local store; bravo-platform encrypts specific Postgres columns selectively.
clusters.consolidationNote: Could share a small @your-org/aes-gcm helper for envelope encryption.

clusters.description: Wrappers around expo-haptics with semantic names.
clusters.similarities: Both wrap expo-haptics behind a domain vocabulary so call sites don't reach for the raw API.
clusters.differences: alpha-app names by intensity (light/medium/heavy); delta-mobile names by intent (confirm/warn/error). delta-mobile's mapping is more opinionated.

clusters.description: Long-running task UI lifecycle with topbar progress.

member.summary: React context with topbar progress beacon and cross-route persistence.

clusters.rationale: The only registered pattern that exposes a generic long-running-task lifecycle to the UI rather than per-call progress callbacks.
clusters.closestRelative: alpha-app/chunked-file-upload exposes per-upload progress, but only inside the upload component — not a reusable lifecycle abstraction.

members.summary: commander-based parser converting kebab-case to camelCase.
init + add commands that generate copy-paste components into the consumer repo.

clusters.similarities: Both treat the CLI as a code-generation surface, not just a runner — kebab-case args map to internal call sites in one, copy-paste component output in the other.
clusters.differences: echo-design-system's CLI emits files into the consumer; charlie-cli's runs to completion and exits.

clusters.description: Optical character recognition by calling Gemini through a Firebase Cloud Function.

member.summary: Direct Gemini OCR call via Cloud Functions.

clusters.rationale: The only OCR pattern in the registry; uses cloud functions specifically to keep API keys off the client.
clusters.closestRelative: alpha-app/multi-provider-ai-router talks to the same provider but for chat, not OCR — different input modality and routing strategy.

member.summary: Five preset palettes + three densities switchable at runtime.

clusters.rationale: echo-design-system is the only project in the registry that ships design tokens; no other registered pattern addresses styling primitives.
clusters.closestRelative: alpha-app/haptic-feedback-abstraction uses presets too, but for a different primitive (haptics, not styling) — surface similarity only.
